title: OstrichPKI EST Server Configuration
description: Configuration file for ostrich-est-server (RFC 7030). Every key is optional; an omitted key falls back to the matching CLI flag / environment variable, then to the built-in default. Precedence: CLI/env > config file > default.

$schema.description: Optional reference to this schema, for editor support.

databaseUrl.description: PostgreSQL connection URL. Prefer supplying this via the DATABASE_URL environment variable rather than committing it to a file.

caGrpcUrl.description: CA gRPC endpoint used for certificate issuance (RFC 7030 §4.2).

caGrpcClientCert.description: Path to the client certificate (PEM) for mTLS to the CA gRPC service.

caGrpcClientKey.description: Path to the client private key (PEM) for mTLS to the CA gRPC service.

caGrpcCaCert.description: Path to the CA certificate (PEM) that verifies the CA gRPC server.

caInsecure.description: DEVELOPMENT ONLY. Allow a plaintext (non-mTLS) gRPC channel to a non-loopback CA endpoint. Never enable in production (NIST 800-53: SC-8, AC-17).

enrollProfile.description: Certificate profile used for enrollment / re-enrollment.

enrollIdentityPolicy.description: How a CSR's requested identity is authorized against the authenticated account (H1). 'username': the CSR must name the account username in its CN or a SAN. 'allowlist': every asserted identity must be in the account's est_account_identities allow-list.

tlsCert.description: Path to the server TLS certificate (PEM).

tlsKey.description: Path to the server TLS private key (PEM).

tlsCaCert.description: Path to the trust anchor (PEM) for mTLS client authentication. Its presence enables mTLS (RFC 7030 §3.3).

allowBasicAuth.description: Accept HTTP Basic authentication as a fallback for bootstrap enrollment (RFC 7030 §3.2.3). Requires tlsCaCert (mTLS).

allowBearerAuth.description: Permit bearer-token authentication when no TLS client CA is configured. Explicit, non-default opt-in to a weaker, non-RFC posture.

mtlsTokenBootstrap.description: Serve token bootstrap and mTLS re-enrollment on one port: request (but do not require) client certificates, authenticate /simplereenroll by the client certificate (RFC 7030 §3.3) and /simpleenroll by a single-use bearer enrollment token. Requires tlsCaCert.

logLevel.description: Log level / filter (e.g. info, debug, ostrich_est=debug).
